title: Tunisian Grassy Tagine
description: A creamy, hangover-cure Tunisian dish featuring dried chillies and a mother sauce base. Perfect for breakfast or brunch.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the dried red chillies and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the medium cauliflower florets and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the cumin, turmeric, and canned tomatoes. Stir well to combine.
Add the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the uncooked rice to the saucepan and stir well to combine.
Cook for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Season with salt to taste and serve hot.
Serve the tagine warm, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
